name: SelectTwo.Html

values.name: preventScrolling
values.comment: Select2 only works when a parent div is not scrollable, this makes parent divs not scrollable while the dropdown is open
values.type: String -> Model b msg -> List ( String, String )

values.name: select2
values.comment:
This the select2 dropdown itself, you pass it a sender and a config and you get back a select2 dropdown. Example:

    let
        testList msg =
            [ ( Just "a", "a" )
            , ( Just "b", "b" )
            , ( Just "c", "c" )
            ]
                |> SelectTwo.basicSelectOptions msg
    in
        select2 SelectTwo
            { defaults =
                (testList Test)
                    |> List.concatMap (\( _, l ) -> l)
                    |> List.filter (\l -> (Just (Test model.test)) == (l |> Tuple3.first))
            , id_ = "test-1"
            , list = testList Test
            , parents = [ "parent" ]
            , clearMsg = Just (\_ -> Test Nothing)
            , width = "300px"
            , placeholder = "Select Test"
            , disabled = False
            , showSearch = True
            , multiSelect = False
            , url = Nothing
            , data = (\_ -> "")
            , processResults = (\( _, params ) -> ( [], params ))
            , delay = 0
            }
values.type: (SelectTwoMsg msg -> msg) -> SelectTwoConfig msg -> Html msg

values.name: select2Close
values.comment: This is an event you put on the body of your elm view, so when a user click away it closes the dropdown

values.name: select2Css
values.comment:
This is a stylesheet link tag to the select2 minified css, use this while developing, but it is more recommended that you use it in your head once
you compile the code instead

values.name: select2Dropdown
values.comment: The dropdown to be shown on the page, this needs to be placed somewhere on the bottome of the view

values.name: widthGuess
values.comment: Use this helper method in the select2 config in order to get select2's width resolve functionality

name: SelectTwo

values.name: basicGroupSelectOptions
values.comment:
turn a list of Tuples3 into a list of GroupSelectTwoOptions with actual grouping. The first parameter is their shared trigger message, the
second parameter is the list of tuples which are formatted as (Key, "Display", "Group Name"), and the return can be used in the list option of the config

    [ ( Just "a", "a", "Group 1")
    , ( Just "b", "b", "Group 1")
    , ( Just "c", "c", "Group 2")
    ]
        |> SelectTwo.basicSelectOptions Test
values.type: (a -> msg) -> List ( a, String, String ) -> List (GroupSelectTwoOption msg)

values.name: basicSelectOptions
values.comment:
turn a list of Tuples into a list of GroupSelectTwoOptions with one group. The first parameter is their shared trigger message, the
second parameter is the list of tuples which are formatted as (Key, "Display"), and the return can be used in the list option of the config

    [ ( Just "a", "a" )
    , ( Just "b", "b" )
    , ( Just "c", "c" )
    ]
        |> SelectTwo.basicSelectOptions Test
values.type: (a -> msg) -> List ( a, String ) -> List (GroupSelectTwoOption msg)

values.name: map
values.comment: modify selectTwo record in your model
values.type: (SelectTwo msg -> SelectTwo msg) -> Model b msg -> Model b msg

values.name: new
values.comment: Create a new instance of the selectTwo record in your model
values.type: List String -> SelectTwoDropdown msg -> Model b msg -> Model b msg

values.name: setSearch
values.comment: set the search parameter in your selectTwo record
values.type: String -> Model b msg -> Model b msg

values.name: update
values.comment:
used in your elm project's update as a way to control the select two boxes on screen it is used by

    yourUpdate : Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    yourUpdate msg model =
        case msg of
            SelectTwo stmsg ->
                update stmsg model
values.type: SelectTwoMsg msg -> Model b msg -> ( Model b msg, Cmd msg )

name: SelectTwo.Types

aliases.name: AjaxParams
aliases.type: { page : Int, term : String, more : Bool, loading : Bool }

aliases.name: GroupSelectTwoOption
aliases.comment: Grouped SelectTwoOption, first option in the tuple is the name of the group

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment: Model structure needed for selectTwo, all records using selectTwo should have this structure

aliases.name: SelectTwo
aliases.comment: Structure created in users model when select2 is activated
aliases.type: { dropdown : SelectTwoDropdown a, hovered : Maybe a, search : Maybe String, parents : List String, list : Maybe (List (GroupSelectTwoOption a)), ajaxStuff : Maybe (SelectTwoAjaxStuff a) }

aliases.name: SelectTwoAjaxStuff
aliases.comment: Things needed to make Ajax calls
aliases.type: ( String, ( String, AjaxParams ) -> String, ( String, AjaxParams ) -> ( List (GroupSelectTwoOption a), AjaxParams ), AjaxParams )

aliases.name: SelectTwoConfig
aliases.comment: Config for SelectTwo used when initializing your select2 box
aliases.type: { defaults : List (SelectTwoOption a), id_ : String, list : List (GroupSelectTwoOption a), parents : List String, url : Maybe String, data : ( String, AjaxParams ) -> String, processResults : ( String, AjaxParams ) -> ( List (GroupSelectTwoOption a), AjaxParams ), clearMsg : Maybe (Maybe a -> a), showSearch : Bool, width : String, placeholder : String, disabled : Bool, multiSelect : Bool, delay : Float }

aliases.name: SelectTwoDropdown
aliases.type: { id_ : String, sender : SelectTwoMsg a -> a, defaults : List (SelectTwoOption a), list : List (GroupSelectTwoOption a), showSearch : Bool, x : Float, y : Float, width : Float, ajaxStuff : Maybe (SelectTwoAjaxStuff a), delay : Float }

aliases.name: SelectTwoOption
aliases.comment: Rows in a select table, first option is the command message to be sent, second is the html to be displayed, and third is the string to search on
